props.message:
Hearthold.

The gate is thrown open. Smoke from the kilns rises to meet the rooks. North: the Sigilkeep. The wardstones still glow there, faintly.

A hand-painted shingle: "Takka the Pebbler. Trades stone for stone. Will not haggle."

The shop is shuttered.

A wax-sealed notice on the smithy door:

"By order of the keepers of the Hold: no sword may be carried past the inner gate. Edge magic flares badly near the sealed wards."

A wax-fresh proclamation:

"By the keepers of Hearthold: any traveler who has drawn breath in the Sourceplain shall be welcomed at the hearths within. Coin is not asked of the brave."

props.open_message: A bundle of warm bread and a heart-flower. The keepers' welcome.

root.speaker: Hearthold Elder
root.text: Well met, little wanderer. The kilns burn warm tonight, and the rooks still keep their watch. Ask, if you would.

source.speaker: Hearthold Elder
source.text: The Source is the spring of all green things — the breath that Lirien gave the world before names were spoken. Glim is a fleck of it, you know. The whole forest is.

khorgaul.speaker: Hearthold Elder
khorgaul.text: The Hoarder. He does not steal so much as he gathers — the light, the warmth, the names of things. The wardstones held him once. They thin now.

sigil.speaker: Hearthold Elder
sigil.text: The Triglyph — three marks, three vows. One for the root, one for the wing, one for the spark. When all three glow, the wards renew. We have lost two.

bye.speaker: Hearthold Elder
bye.text: Walk under the wing of Lirien, friend.

root.speaker: Takka the Smith
root.text: Eh? You look light in the pouch, traveler. Need any pebbles for the sling?

give.speaker: Takka the Smith
give.text: Five pebbles. Good for one good throw and four bad ones. Don't tell the Pebbler.

post_give.speaker: Takka the Smith
post_give.text: Mind the wardstones to the north. They sing when they're upset.

already_gave.speaker: Takka the Smith
already_gave.text: Aye, and I'll not give again 'til the moon turns. Off with you, sling-arm.

root.speaker: Pim
root.text: Lambwisp! LAMBWISP! Did you see one? It went *floof* and then it went *poof*!

two.speaker: Pim
two.text: Mama says they're little pieces of Lirien's breath. I want to catch one and put it in a jar. Don't tell mama.

root.speaker: Father Velis
root.text: Welcome to the Hearth-shrine, traveler. The kilns warm our bones; the Source warms our names. You bring questions. Speak them.

source.speaker: Father Velis
source.text: The Source flows through every green thing — through fire as it leaps, through water as it falls, through the breath of any kin who tells the truth. To break it is the easiest thing. To mend it asks a life.

trig.speaker: Father Velis
trig.text: Three sigils, three vows. Sight — to see truly. Shaping — to make and unmake. Sharing — to refuse the hoard. The first two were claimed in elder days. The third was given to a small one. Perhaps to you.

lirien.speaker: Father Velis
lirien.text: Lirien the Maker, the Wing, the Greenhand. We say she is everywhere a leaf unfurls and nowhere we may name. Her gift is the giving — the freedom of the green to grow and grow again.

khor.speaker: Father Velis
khor.text: Khorgaul the Hoarder, the Cold-hand, the Without-Sharing. He is what the Source becomes when nothing is given. We do not curse him at the shrine. We pity him. And we hold the wards.

shrine.speaker: Father Velis
shrine.text: The hearth-shrine warms two flames — one for the kiln, one for the kin. When both burn, Hearthold endures. Should one go cold, the other will not last the season.

bless.speaker: Father Velis
bless.text:
*marks your brow with ash*
Go with the wing of Lirien at your back. Go with the green underfoot. Go with the giving in your hand.

root.speaker: Tilly
root.text: HYAA! I am the bravest knight in Hearthold and I have THREE swords and they are ALL invisible except this one which is a stick.

two.speaker: Tilly
two.text:
Wanna duel? *brandishes stick*
No? Fine. I'll save the realm without you. Tell Father Velis if I don't come back by supper.

props.open_message: Tucked in the kitchen-yard behind the Elder's house. The keepers leave heart-pulses where children can find them first.

props.message: Worn cellar steps disappear under the kitchen-yard. The Elder's pickles, perhaps.
